When we look around, we see a world full of evil people who prosper and live comfortably. This has been seen before, so let’s not be surprised by it. We are tempted to worry about this, to think they are the only happy people, and we may be prone to do like them; but we are warned against that. Outward success is temporary. When we look forward with faith, we will see no reason to envy those who do wrong. Their weeping and wailing will last forever. The life of religion involves trusting in the Lord and diligently working to serve him according to his will. It is tempting God if we don’t make an effort to fulfill our duty to him. A person's life does not consist in abundance, but you shall have what is convenient for you. This is more than we deserve, and it is enough for someone going to heaven. Finding joy in God is as much a privilege as it is a duty. He has not promised to satisfy the desires of the body or the whims of imagination, but the desires of the renewed, sanctified soul. What does a good person desire? They want to know, love, and serve God. Commit your way to the Lord; roll your way upon the Lord, as the margin reads it. Cast your burden on the Lord, the burden of your care. We must release it from ourselves, not trouble or confuse ourselves with thoughts about future events, but instead refer them to God. By prayer, present your case and all your cares before the Lord and trust in him. We must do our duty and then leave the outcome with God. The promise is very comforting: He will bring that to pass, whatever it is, which you have committed to him.

Let us be content that God will make everything work out for our good. Let us not become upset by what we see in this world. A fretful and discontented spirit is open to many temptations. In every respect, the little that is given to the righteous is more comfortable and more profitable than the ill-gotten and abused riches of ungodly people. It comes from a hand of special love. God provides abundantly and well, not only for his working servants but also for his waiting servants. They have something better than wealth: peace of mind, peace with God, and then peace in God; that peace the world cannot give and which the world cannot possess. God knows the believer’s days. Not one day's work will go unrewarded. Their time on earth is measured by days, which will soon be numbered; but heavenly happiness will last forever. This will be a real comfort to believers in difficult times. Those who rest on the Rock of ages have no reason to envy the wicked their support from broken reeds.

The Lord our God requires that we act justly and render to all what is due. It is a great sin for those who are able to deny payment of just debts; it is a great misery not to be able to pay them. Whoever is truly merciful will always be merciful. We must abandon our sins, learn to do well, and hold fast to it. This is true religion. The blessing of God is the source, sweetness, and security of all earthly pleasures. And if we are sure of this, we can be sure not to lack anything good in this world. By his grace and Holy Spirit, he directs the thoughts, affections, and plans of good people. By his providence, he overrules events so as to make their path clear. He does not always show them his way for a distance, but leads them step by step, as children are led. God will keep them from being ruined by their failures, whether into sin or trouble, though those who fall into sin will be severely harmed. Few, if any, have known a consistent believer or his children reduced to complete poverty and without friends. God does not abandon his faithful people in times of distress; and only the righteous will live forever in heaven—that will be their eternal home. A good person may fall into the hands of a messenger of Satan and be severely afflicted, but God will not leave him in his enemy’s power.

Duty is ours, and we must attend to it; but events are God's, and we must refer the handling of them to him. What a striking picture is in verse #(, ), of many a prosperous enemy of God! But God remarkably frustrates the plans of the prosperous wicked, especially persecutors. No one is perfect in themselves, but believers are complete in Christ Jesus. Even if all of a saint's days continue dark and cloudy, his dying day may prove comfortable, and his sun set brightly; or, if it should set under a cloud, then his future state will be everlasting peace. The salvation of the righteous will be the Lord’s doing. He will help them to do their duties, bear their burdens, endure their troubles well, gain good from them, and in due time deliver them out of their troubles. Let sinners depart from evil and do good; repent of and abandon sin, and trust in the mercy of God through Jesus Christ. Let them take his yoke upon themselves and learn from him so that they may live forevermore in heaven. Let us observe the ending scenes of different people, and always depend on God’s mercy.
